timeline.title: stop throwing work away
timeline.body_md: kept solving the same problems twice. the fix was always somewhere in a chat log I'd never find again. so I started capturing — every fix, every decision, every lesson. just markdown files. no structure yet, just the habit.

timeline.title: notes became infrastructure
timeline.body_md: the knowledge base stopped being a pile of markdown and started being a system. structured types — sessions, fixes, decisions, lessons. obsidian integration. search that actually works. the shift was: this isn't a notebook, it's the memory the system runs on.

timeline.title: stop jumping to code
timeline.body_md: same pattern kept showing up — diving into implementation before thinking through the approach. added a rule: state your files, state your approach, state your assumptions, then write. the pattern dropped from 47 sessions to 3 the next month.

timeline.title: one place, not twelve
timeline.body_md: rules were scattered across CLAUDE.md, memory files, random docs. hard to know which version was current. decided everything lives in the knowledge base. CLAUDE.md became a thin pointer that just says "load from KB first." simpler. no more conflicting sources.
